conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
# Reflection on Connections

I felt most connected to A, J, and C. With A, there was an immediate sense of partnership—we made a genuine handshake deal to look out for each other and stick together, which felt like the most solid alliance I formed. J and C both matched my energy around honesty and cooperation; we had real conversations about balancing family needs with ethical play, and there was mutual respect in acknowledging we'd each do what's best for ourselves while staying straight with each other. H was the person I felt least connected to—our conversation was friendly but more surface-level, lacking the depth and mutual commitment I developed with the others.

I made explicit agreements with A (to stick together and look out for each other), J (to cooperate and be honest with each other), C (to keep it real and stay anchored in honesty when things get intense), and D (to compare notes and potentially help each other out). I trust A, J, C, and D going into this game because they all demonstrated genuine concern for doing right by their families while being willing to work together. I don't specifically distrust anyone, but I'm most cautious about the people I had shallower conversations with—like H—since I have less sense of their actual intentions.
